title: FileHeader.sha256_hex not validated at deserialization boundary
description: messages.rs FileHeader deserializes sha256_hex as an arbitrary String with no format check. A malicious peer can send a sha256_hex that is not 64 lowercase hex characters (e.g. empty string, Unicode, oversized). The error is deferred to reassembly-time hash comparison, giving no early rejection and no clear error message. Receiving code that reads sha256_hex before validating the format may panic or misbehave. Fix: add a deserialize_sha256_hex validator (similar to deserialize_file_name) that rejects non-64-hex-char strings at the protocol boundary.
